description: Marketing agencies, creative studios, development shops, and consultancies typically with 3-50 people. Characterized by project-based work with values ranging from $1K-$10K, thin margins (15-20%), and owner-driven fast decision making. High sensitivity to cash flow and immediate ROI requirements.

pain_points.pain: Thin margins (15-20%) leaving no room for tool investments
pain_points.context: Agencies operate on tight margins with pressure from both clients and costs
Clients continuously expand requirements without corresponding budget increases

pain_points.pain: Cash flow volatility from project-based revenue
pain_points.context: Feast or famine cycles make subscription commitments feel risky

pain_points.pain: Clients unwilling to pay for tools passed through in billing
pain_points.context: Clients question any line items beyond direct labor
Clients believe AI can replace agency services or demand AI-powered pricing

pain_points.pain: Team utilization and billable rate optimization
pain_points.context: Balancing capacity across projects while maximizing billable hours

buying_triggers: Losing a major client representing 20%+ of revenue
Client budget cuts requiring efficiency improvements
Ad platform policy changes affecting service delivery
Cash flow crisis requiring operational changes
Scope creep incidents destroying multiple project margins
Competitor agencies winning deals with new capabilities
budget_cycle: Project-based budgeting with quarterly reviews. Discretionary spending often tied to new client wins or retained earnings. Strong preference for monthly subscriptions over annual commitments due to cash flow concerns.
regulatory_concerns: Data privacy for client customer data

terminology.definition: Recurring monthly fee for ongoing services, providing predictable revenue
terminology.usage_context: Revenue model. 'We have 8 clients on monthly retainers averaging $5K.'

terminology.definition: Statement of Work - document defining project scope, deliverables, timeline, and cost
terminology.usage_context: Contract document. 'We need to finalize the SOW before kicking off.'

terminology.definition: Pricing based on value delivered rather than time spent
terminology.usage_context: Pricing strategy. 'We moved to value-based pricing for strategy work.'

terminology.definition: Gradual expansion of project requirements beyond original agreement
terminology.usage_context: Project risk. 'Scope creep killed our margin on that project.'

terminology.definition: Formal request to modify scope with associated budget adjustment
terminology.usage_context: Process. 'Any additions need to go through a change order.'

terminology.definition: Percentage of available time spent on billable work
terminology.usage_context: Efficiency metric. 'Our target utilization rate is 75%.'

terminology.definition: Hourly rate charged to clients for work performed
Return on Ad Spend - revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising
terminology.usage_context: Performance metric. 'We delivered 4x ROAS on their Meta campaigns.'

terminology.definition: Marketing Efficiency Ratio - total revenue divided by total marketing spend
terminology.usage_context: Efficiency metric. 'Their MER improved from 3x to 5x under our management.'

terminology.definition: Software resold under the agency's brand to clients
terminology.usage_context: Revenue model. 'We white-label their dashboard and charge clients $500/month.'

terminology.definition: Revenue sharing arrangement where compensation is tied to client results
terminology.usage_context: Pricing model. 'We do rev-share deals with e-commerce clients.'

terminology.definition: Rate at which clients end their relationship with the agency
terminology.usage_context: Retention metric. 'Our client churn is 15% annually.'

terminology.definition: Average hourly rate across team members of different seniority levels
terminology.usage_context: Pricing. 'Our blended rate for the project team is $125/hour.'

terminology.definition: Initial paid engagement to understand requirements before main project
terminology.usage_context: Sales process. 'We always start with a paid discovery phase.'

common_objections.objection: Our margins are too thin to add another subscription
common_objections.underlying_concern: Every cost directly impacts already slim profit margins
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show direct margin improvement from efficiency gains. Calculate break-even in billable hours saved. Position as investment with measurable ROI within 30-60 days.

common_objections.objection: Clients won't pay for this - it'll come out of our margin
common_objections.underlying_concern: Agency can't pass through costs and absorbs all tool expenses
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show how to build into project pricing invisibly. Demonstrate client-facing value that justifies higher rates. Provide white-label options.

common_objections.objection: We already have too many subscriptions we barely use
common_objections.underlying_concern: Subscription fatigue and concern about adding to software bloat
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Audit their current stack and show consolidation opportunity. Demonstrate immediate daily usage value. Offer to replace existing tools.

common_objections.objection: This will just lead to more scope creep with clients
common_objections.underlying_concern: New capabilities create client expectations without budget increases
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Show how tool actually helps document and enforce scope. Provide change order templates. Demonstrate how it supports value-based pricing.

common_objections.objection: I can't commit to annual pricing with our cash flow
common_objections.underlying_concern: Project-based revenue makes long-term commitments risky
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Offer monthly pricing option. Show how tool actually improves cash flow through efficiency. Provide flexible pause options during slow periods.

common_objections.objection: My team won't actually use this
common_objections.underlying_concern: Past tool purchases have gone unused after initial enthusiasm
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Provide adoption playbook specific to agencies. Offer team training. Show usage data from similar agencies. Tie to specific daily workflow.

common_objections.objection: With AI, clients expect everything to be faster and cheaper
common_objections.underlying_concern: AI is commoditizing agency services and compressing rates
common_objections.effective_response_approach: Position tool as way to capture AI efficiency gains for agency rather than client. Show how to reposition services as higher-value. Demonstrate AI-augmented premium offerings.

typical_incumbent_solutions: Spreadsheets for project management and tracking
Basic project management tools (Asana, Monday, Trello)
Platform-native tools (Meta Business Suite, Google Analytics)
switching_barriers: Client reporting dependencies on current tools
Historical data and benchmarks in current systems
Low switching costs but high adoption friction

scenario_templates.template: {{agency_type}} agency owner with {{team_size}} team members just lost a client representing {{revenue_percent}}% of revenue. They need to {{goal}} to survive the next {{timeline}}.
{{role}} at a {{agency_type}} agency is dealing with {{pain_point}}. They have {{num_clients}} clients and their current utilization rate is {{utilization}}%. Budget for tools is {{budget}}/month.

A {{agency_size}} {{agency_type}} agency is scaling from {{current_size}} to {{target_size}} people. The owner needs to improve {{operational_area}} before growth creates chaos. Decision will be made by {{decision_maker}}.
{{role}} runs a {{agency_type}} agency and clients are pressuring them about AI. They're worried about {{ai_concern}} and looking for ways to {{goal}}.
